title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Hybrid Migration · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds post-quantum inventory and hybrid migration plan, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of post-quantum inventory and hybrid migration plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time.
Use Go ML-KEM interfaces for key encapsulation with strict key/ciphertext parsing, shared-key custody, failure handling, and hybrid context.
Use Go ML-DSA parameter sets and context-aware signing with explicit key, signature-size, interoperability, and policy tradeoffs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Quantum Threat Inventory
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 2687 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate hybrid ML-KEM and ML-DSA migration on inventory, specified combiner, transcript binding, downgrade defense, and rollback; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Quantum Threat Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Quantum Threat Inventory, keep Crypto Go Quantum Threat Inventory connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
